back_translation: Come now, you rich, weep and wail loudly because of the misery that is coming upon you
Your wealth has rotted, and your clothes have been eaten by insects
Your gold and silver have rusted, and the rust will be a witness against you and consume your flesh like fire. You have hoarded treasure in the last days
Behold, the wages of the workers who harvested in your fields, which you have withheld, are crying out, and the cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of Hosts
You have lived luxuriously on the earth and indulged yourselves. You have fattened your hearts in the day of slaughter
You have condemned and killed the righteous one. He did not resist you
Therefore, brothers, be patient until the coming of the Lord. Behold, the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth, patiently waiting until he receives the early rain and the late rain
You also be patient, strengthen your hearts, because the coming of the Lord has drawn near
Brothers, do not grumble against one another, so that you will not be judged. Behold, the Judge is standing at the door
Brothers, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord as an example of patience in suffering
Behold, we count those who have endured as blessed. You have heard of Job’s endurance, and you have seen the outcome that the Lord gave — that the Lord is full of compassion and mercy
My brothers, above all, do not swear, neither by heaven nor by earth, nor by any other oath. But let your “Yes” be yes, and your “No” be no, so that you do not fall under judgment
Is anyone among you suffering? Let that person pray. Is anyone cheerful? Let that person sing songs of praise
Is anyone among you sick? Let that person call the elders of the church, and let them pray for him and anoint him with oil in the name of the Lord
And the prayer offered in faith will save the sick person and make him well, and the Lord will raise him up. If he has committed sins, his sins will be forgiven
Therefore, confess your sins to one another, and pray for one another, so that you may be healed. The working prayer of a righteous person has great power
Elijah was a person with feelings just like ours. He prayed earnestly that it would not rain, and rain did not fall on the land for three years and six months
He prayed again, and the heavens gave rain, and the earth produced its crops
My brothers, if anyone among you wanders from the truth, and someone brings him back
let him know that whoever brings a sinner back from the way of his wandering will save his soul from death and will cover a multitude of sins
